name: arms apart circular toe touch (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended to the sides.
Keeping your legs straight, bend forward at the waist and reach down towards your toes with your right hand.
As you reach down, simultaneously lift your left leg straight up behind you, maintaining balance.
Return to the starting position and repeat the movement with your left hand reaching towards your toes and your right leg lifting up behind you.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended to the sides. Keeping your legs straight, bend forward at the waist and reach down towards your toes with your right hand. As you reach down, simultaneously lift your left leg straight up behind you, maintaining balance. Return to the starting position and repeat the movement with your left hand reaching towards your toes and your right leg lifting up behind you. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Single-leg or balance-intensive variations increase fall risk. Use support or a seated alternative.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
